FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class CalendarView

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI CalendarView: a header row (a HeaderButton that ZOOMS Month→Year→Decade + EDDB/EDDC caret nav buttons), a 1px divider, then the active view — a localized weekday header over a fixed 6-week day grid (Month), a 4-column month grid (Year) or a 4-column year grid (Decade) — cross-zooming with the WinUI 150/500ms scale-fade transition (CalendarView_themeresources.xaml:496-528). Today is an accent-FILLED circle; selected days carry a 1px accent RING with accent text; out-of-scope (adjacent-month) days render in secondary text and navigate+select on click. Selection is signal-based (SelectedDate) with None/Single/Multiple modes and Min/MaxDate bounds.

public sealed class CalendarView : Component

Fields

DisplayMode

The initial zoom level (the header button / item picks navigate it afterwards).

public CalendarViewDisplayMode DisplayMode

Field Value

CalendarViewDisplayMode

FirstDayOfWeek

Null = the culture's first day of week (CalendarView_Partial.cpp:2275-2286; Sunday in en-US).

public DayOfWeek? FirstDayOfWeek

Field Value

DayOfWeek?

IsOutOfScopeEnabled

False renders adjacent-month cells blank (WinUI IsOutOfScopeEnabled).

NumberOfWeeksInView

int

OnSelectedDatesChanged

WinUI SelectedDatesChanged: invoked with the post-change selected dates (empty on deselect).

public Action<IReadOnlyList<DateOnly>>? OnSelectedDatesChanged

Field Value

Action<IReadOnlyList<DateOnly>>

SelectedDate

Caller-owned single-selection date (two-way); ignored in Multiple mode. Null = no selection.

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
